symptoms.symptom_name: Paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnoea — woken from sleep gasping, must sit bolt upright

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: PND is a classic feature of left-ventricular failure — redistribution of fluid on lying flat floods the alveoli.
Nocturnal wheeze occurs in asthma, but the strict orthopnoea/PND pattern relieved only by sitting upright points to cardiac failure.
Frothy pink sputum reflects protein-rich, blood-tinged fluid in flooded alveoli — highly specific for pulmonary oedema.
Pneumonic sputum is typically purulent/rusty, not frothy and pink.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profound orthopnoea — dramatic worsening on lying flat

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Orthopnoea is a cardinal symptom of raised left-atrial pressure and pulmonary congestion.
Sympathetic activation and poor peripheral perfusion accompany hypertensive acute heart failure and mark severity.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chest tightness with a sense of impending doom

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chest tightness, diaphoresis and impending doom raise ACS as a precipitant requiring ECG/troponin.

symptoms.symptom_name: Missed regular furosemide dose and unworn CPAP overnight

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A missed diuretic and an unused CPAP mask are classic precipitants of overnight decompensation into acute pulmonary oedema.
Peripheral oedema reflects chronic volume overload / right-sided involvement in decompensated heart failure.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral fine inspiratory crackles from the bases to the mid-zones (auscultation)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fine bibasal-to-midzone inspiratory crackles indicate alveolar fluid; sealed clinician finding — the AI Doctor cannot auscultate.
Airways disease produces expiratory polyphonic wheeze, not fine inspiratory crackles.
An S3 gallop is highly specific for raised filling pressures and left-ventricular failure; sealed clinician finding.
Raised JVP indicates right-sided fluid overload; sealed clinician finding not obtainable via chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Oxygen saturation 88 percent on room air (clinician-measured)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Severe hypoxia confirms impending respiratory failure; measured by the clinician's monitor and sealed — the AI infers severity from exhaustion and accessory-muscle use, not a measured number.
Hypertensive acute heart failure — the afterload surge that precipitated the flash oedema; sealed measured vitals.

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Loss of biochemical confirmation of heart failure — but treatment is clinical and must not wait for the result.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Elevated BNP supports cardiac failure as the cause of the breathlessness; hospital investigation, never available in real-time telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chest radiograph — cardiomegaly, prominent upper-lobe vessels, Kerley B lines

investigation_details.expected_result: Cardiomegaly, upper-lobe venous diversion, Kerley B lines, interstitial/alveolar oedema
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Delayed radiographic confirmation — but does not change the immediate emergency management.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Classic radiographic signs of cardiogenic pulmonary oedema; sealed ED investigation.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Profound orthopnoea, frothy pink sputum, bilateral fine crackles and an S3 gallop together are effectively diagnostic of acute cardiogenic pulmonary oedema.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Identify an acute coronary syndrome or arrhythmia precipitating the pulmonary oedema — done by paramedics/ED, not the AI Doctor.
Exclude an ACS precipitant of flash pulmonary oedema.
Confirm pulmonary oedema and cardiomegaly; ED investigation.
Biochemical support for heart failure; does not delay treatment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Nebulised bronchodilator trial as a diagnostic step
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Do not treat this as reversible airways disease — a bronchodilator does not address alveolar flooding and delays offloading and oxygen support.
